title: Oxandra martiana
taxonomy: Oxandra martiana is classified within the family Annonaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. This family is part of the order Magnoliales, a group of flowering plants that are often woody and have a distinctive flower structure. The Annonaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants, with over 2,000 species spread across the globe. Oxandra martiana is a unique species within this family, with its own set of characteristics and growth habits. By understanding its classification, researchers can gain insights into its evolutionary history and relationships with other species.
